posts.name: Robert Pavlovich
posts.cooked:
Being a bit of a skeptic of 2015 Burgundy from the outset, this wine made me realize there are serious treasures to be found. It has to be a perfect (non-Leroy) Vosne Romanee Beaux Monts…at least for those that like their red Burgundy nice and savory and with a touch of sweetness. There is a good mix of dark red and black fruit with old vine sap, and complex spice. With plenty of acidic freshness to meet the just ripe fruit, it begs to be drunk and savored.

Warning: This was consumed on June 8th and may have shut down since. Also can’t vouch for Desaunay-Bissey across the board in 15’ as I found their Echezeaux to be nice, but not as satisfying, and their Grands Echezeaux to be disappointing.
Very interesting Robert. I’ve never even heard of the producer. (Not surprising perhaps, as I’m a relative burgundy ignoramus).
I have a few stray 05s from this producer. Who is currently importing?

posts.name: Robert Pavlovich
posts.cooked:
Very interesting Robert. I’ve never even heard of the producer. (Not surprising perhaps, as I’m a relative burgundy ignoramus).

They are very far under the radar, especially in the US market. Hadn’t heard of them at the beginning of the year and I just bought one of these by chance, looking at the right email at the right time. Also heard the winemaker, Bruno Desaunay Bidsey, makes some of Dominique Laurent’s wines.

JC…as Grand Vin Wine Merchants is the only retailer with it and I know they do their share of direct importing, I’d guess they brought it in.

Very interesting Robert. I’ve never even heard of the producer. (Not surprising perhaps, as I’m a relative burgundy ignoramus).

They are very far under the radar, especially in the US market. Hadn’t heard of them at the beginning of the year and I just bought one of these by chance, looking at the right email at the right time. Also heard the winemaker, Bruno Desaunay Bidsey, makes some of Dominique Laurent’s wines.

JC…as Grand Vin Wine Merchants is the only retailer with it and I know they do their share of direct importing, I’d guess they brought it in.

Your enthusiasm sent me to wine-searcher and saw it at GVWM. Also saw it was $100 wine and passed.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
I have a few stray 05s from this producer. Who is currently importing?

Weygandt.

posts.name: Robert Pavlovich
posts.cooked:

Weygandt.

Oh right, I did see their label.

Did you publish a TN for this wine William?

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
I have a few stray 05s from this producer. Who is currently importing?

Weygandt.

Oh right, I did see their label.

Did you publish a TN for this wine William?

I did! Thought it was very solid. Though I had more time for the Echezeaux than you did. I have never visited the domaine and don’t know much about it, will be looking them up some time soon.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: Bought some 99s. Were all good. May have an Echezeaux left. Haven’t seen them for some time.

I have a few stray 05s from this producer. Who is currently importing?

Weygandt.

Thanks, William. For some reason I thought they parted ways, but I was mistaken.

posts.name: Bertrand Leulliette
posts.cooked: Glad to report that I have taken over Importation of these jewels… still flying under the radar and making plenty of customers happy!!!

posts.name: Joshua Kates
posts.cooked: I have this same exact bottling. You sure that Envoyer did not push it out? In any case, I’m glad to hear it’s showing so well. Not to be an a-hole about it, I was planning to apply the 15-year rule, but definitely good to know.
